summary: The Gradle build failed because one unit test failed: DbVersionTest.testCollectionVersion reported an unexpected assertion (owner version incremented).
root_cause: The test org.hibernate.orm.test.version.db.DbVersionTest.testCollectionVersion failed with an assertion: owner version was incremented when the test expected it not to be.

evidence.reason: Shows which test failed in the test output.

evidence.quote: org.opentest4j.AssertionFailedError: owner version not incremented ==> expected: <false> but was: <true>
evidence.reason: The assertion failure message from the failing test, describing the exact expectation vs actual.
Summary indicating there was exactly one test failure in the entire test run.
The Gradle task failed because tests failed, causing the build to stop.

evidence.quote: There were failing tests. See the report at: file:///home/runner/work/hibernate-orm/hibernate-orm/hibernate-core/target/reports/tests/test/index.html
evidence.reason: Points to the test report that contains details for the failing test.

suggested_fix: Reproduce and inspect the failing test locally: rerun the single test and open the test report at hibernate-core/target/reports/tests/test/index.html. Examine org.hibernate.orm.test.version.db.DbVersionTest (see the assertion at line referenced in the stack trace) to understand why the owner version is being incremented (expected false but was true). If behavior changed in code, either adjust the implementation to preserve the expected versioning semantics, or update the test expectation if the new behavior is correct. Run the isolated test repeatedly to confirm the fix before re-running the full test suite.
